name: White Tārā Practice (Long-Life Sādhana)
name_sanskrit: Sītātārā — Oṃ Tāre Tuttāre Ture Mama Āyuḥ Puṇya Jñāna Puṣṭiṃ Kuru Svāhā
primary_purpose: Extension of lifespan; healing of illness; cultivation of compassionate serenity; removal of obstacles to longevity for self and all beings

mantras.use: Primary White Tārā long-life mantra — the seven-syllable Goddess who dispels all illness and extends life

mantra_short: Oṃ Tāre Tuttāre Ture Mama Āyuḥ Puṇya Jñāna Puṣṭiṃ Kuru Svāhā
mantra_sanskrit: Oṃ Tāre Tuttāre Ture Mama Āyuḥ Puṇya Jñāna Puṣṭiṃ Kuru Svāhā
visualizations: White Tārā appears in the space before you, peaceful and smiling, seated on a white moon disc atop a pink lotus throne
Her body is radiant luminous white (#F5F5FF), the color of fresh snow and pure moonlight — embodying serenity, healing, and deathless wisdom
She has seven eyes: the two usual eyes, plus one in each palm, one in each sole of her foot, and a wisdom eye on her forehead — seeing all suffering in every realm with impartial compassion

Her right hand rests on her right knee in the mudrā of supreme giving (varada); her left hand is held at her heart in the mudrā of the Three Jewels, holding the stem of a white utpala lotus that blossoms at her shoulder
She is adorned with silken robes, jeweled ornaments, and her hair is bound in a topknot; she sits in vajra posture, fully awakened

From her seven eyes and her heart, streams of white healing nectar and moonlight pour into your crown, filling your entire body with radiant white light — dissolving all illness, aging, and obstacles to long life into pure luminous awareness
The white light then radiates outward to all sentient beings, relieving their illness and extending their lives so they may practice the Dharma and attain awakening
benefits: Extension of lifespan and removal of obstacles to longevity
Healing of physical and mental illness, especially chronic conditions
Restoration and stabilization of vital energy (prāṇa / lung)
Cultivation of compassionate serenity, like the cool light of the full moon
Purification of the karma of killing and harming that shortens life
Development of the seven qualities of a long-life practitioner: merit, wisdom, compassion, healing power, fearlessness, radiance, and deathlessness
Connection to the Mother of All Buddhas in her specifically healing, longevity-granting emanation
Blessing for those who are sick, elderly, or near death — relieves their suffering
frequency_purpose: Solfeggio frequency for facilitating change and dissolving negative patterns — supports the karmic purification that allows life-extension
element: Water (cooling, healing, flowing nectar of compassion)
seven_eyes_meaning: Two eyes — see the conventional world of appearances
Two palm eyes — see and heal the actions of body
Two foot eyes — see and guide the path of all beings
Forehead wisdom eye — sees all past, present, and future with non-dual wisdom
best_times: Daily at dawn or dusk, traditionally recited 108 times
On White Tārā's feast days (especially the 25th lunar day — Dakini Day, and the full moon)
During illness, recovery, or when longevity feels threatened
For the long life of one's spiritual teachers, parents, and loved ones
At times of astrological danger to life (bardo periods, obstacle years)
On behalf of those who are dying, to ease their passage and extend opportunities for Dharma
dedication: By the merit of reciting White Tārā's long-life mantra, may I and all beings be freed from illness, premature death, and all obstacles to longevity. May our lives be long, our health stable, and our wisdom vast — that we may attain awakening for the benefit of all. Oṃ Tāre Tuttāre Ture Mama Āyuḥ Puṇya Jñāna Puṣṭiṃ Kuru Svāhā.
